# Reconciliation: Alloy models vs Dafny models

## Summary

| Formalism | File | Verifier result |
|---|---|---|
| Alloy | `planning.als` | 13/13 checks pass; 11 SAT runs + 2 expected-UNSAT |
| Alloy | `planning_lease.als` | 6/6 checks pass; 5 SAT runs + 2 expected-UNSAT |
| Alloy | `planning_plan_race.als` | 1/1 check pass; 1 expected-UNSAT |
| Dafny | `planning.dfy` | 14/14 lemmas proved; 23 functions well-formed |
| Dafny | `planning_plan_race.dfy` | 5/5 lemmas proved |

Alloy total: **20/20 checks pass.** Dafny total: **19/19 lemmas pass.**

The chain_predecessor migration was mechanism-agnostic — neither model needed structural change because both abstract claim-race resolution as `commitClaim | abortClaim`, leaving the storage-level enforcement detail under the abstraction. The lease-model extension this session (heartbeat-vs-reclaim race) added two new safety properties to Alloy and is the third Alloy-only family that hasn't been ported to Dafny.

## Per-property cross-check

| Property | Alloy | Dafny | Equivalent? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Static invariant: `pending ↔ phase`, owner only after claim, terminal implies proof, cancelled implies rejected+proof, verifier-required done implies `VerifierPassed` | `fact`s in `planning.als` | `Inv` + `InvAlwaysHolds` in `planning.dfy` | **Yes** |
| Terminal absorption | `TerminalAbsorbing` | `TerminalAbsorbing` | **Yes** |
| Proof required for terminal | `ProofRequiredForTerminal` | `ProofRequiredForTerminal` | **Yes** |
| Single owner / working has owner | `SingleOwner`, `OwnerStableThroughWorkingPhase` | implicit via `owner: map<int,int>` + `Inv` predicate | **Yes** (structural in Dafny — a `map` cannot have two values for one key) |
| Claim-race safety: at most one committed attempt per task | `NoDoubleCommit` | `NoDoubleCommitPerTask` | **Yes** |
| Dependency gate before claim | `DependenciesDoneAtClaim` | `DependenciesDoneAtClaim` | **Yes** |
| **Cycle / self-loop refusal at plan time** (new) | `plan[t]` precondition `t not in t.deps`; `fact NoCycle` annotated as derived | **NOT MODELED** as an explicit precondition; Dafny's `StepPlan` has no equivalent guard | **No — Alloy ahead (small)** |
| Verifier gate on `done` | `VerifierGateOnDone` | `VerifierGateOnDone` | **Yes** |
| Verify only on working+report | `VerifyRequiresWorkingReport` | `VerifyRequiresWorkingReport` | **Yes** |
| Cancel only on non-terminal | `CancelOnlyOnNonTerminal` | `CancelOnlyOnNonTerminal` | **Yes** |
| Cancelled tasks carry proof | `CancelledHasProof` | `CancelledHasProof` | **Yes** |
| Cancelled is rejected & terminal-absorbing | `CancelledIsRejectedTerminal` (dedicated assertion) | held via `Inv` (`cancelled[t] ⇒ phase[t]==PRejected`) + `TerminalAbsorbing`, **no dedicated lemma** | **Yes (semantically)**, **no (presentationally)** |
| Fairness/liveness: open attempts eventually stop being open | `AttemptProgress` fact | `FairTrace` + `FairOpenAttemptEventuallyLeavesOpenSet` | **Yes**, both as a fairness assumption |
| First disappearance is a real resolution step | implicit in `commitClaim`/`abortClaim` split | `OpenAttemptCanDisappearOnlyByResolution` + `FirstDisappearanceIsResolution` | **Yes** |
| Slug uniqueness across pending tasks | `UniqueSlugInQueue` in `planning_plan_race.als` | `UniqueSlugInQueue` in `planning_plan_race.dfy` | **Yes** |
| **Sticky-chain coherence** (per-chain single-context binding) | `StickyChainCoherence`, `StickyBindingOnlyAtClaim` (+ `Sticky` sig) | **NOT MODELED** | **No — Alloy ahead** |
| **Lease / reclaim safety** (`Alive` + crash + reclaim) | `planning_lease.als`: `SingleOwner`, `LiveWorkerActions`, `NoZombieAfterReclaim` | **NOT MODELED** | **No — Alloy ahead, no Dafny port exists** |
| **Heartbeat-vs-reclaim race** (new this session) | `planning_lease.als`: `LiveHeartbeatBlocksReclaim`, `ReclaimRequiresStableHeartbeatChain` (+ `HbSinceObs` flag, `heartbeat`/`sweepObserve` transitions) | **NOT MODELED** | **No — Alloy ahead, no Dafny lease port exists** |

**Aligned: 13/17 shared properties.** Four Alloy-only families (cycle/self-loop refusal, sticky context, lease/reclaim, heartbeat-vs-reclaim race) are not in Dafny.

## Discrepancies

### 1. Cycle / self-loop refusal — Alloy has, Dafny doesn't (new this session)
- **Alloy evidence**: `planning.als` `plan[t]` carries the precondition `t not in t.deps`; `fact NoCycle` is annotated as derived from this + Task immutability.
- **Dafny evidence**: `planning.dfy:StepPlan` has no equivalent guard; the static invariant `Inv` doesn't constrain `info[t].deps`.
- **Outcome**: **FixModel (Dafny — small)** — add a `t !in info[t].deps` requires-clause to `StepPlan` and a derived lemma showing `t !in info[t].deps^*` over valid traces.
- **Code evidence**: `plan.py` exit 11 with three sub-cases; `tests/integration/test_golden.py:G18,G19`.
- **Effort**: Trivial (~10 LOC).

### 2. Sticky-context safety — Alloy has, Dafny doesn't (carried forward)
- **Alloy evidence**: `planning.als:61` declares `sig Sticky in Task {}`; `planning.als:399-416` proves `StickyChainCoherence` and `StickyBindingOnlyAtClaim` for 4 tasks / 10 steps.
- **Dafny evidence**: `planning.dfy` has no notion of stickiness — `TaskInfo` carries only `deps` and `requiresVerifier`. `StepCommitClaim` doesn't condition on context.
- **Outcome**: **FixModel (Dafny lags)**.
- **Direction**: Extend `TaskInfo` with `sticky: bool`, add a `contextId: map<int, int>` field per task, constrain `StepCommitClaim` to require sticky-ancestor `contextId` matches, and prove `StickyChainCoherence` as an `Inv` clause + `StickyBindingOnlyAtClaim` as a transition lemma.
- **Effort**: Medium (~80 LOC).

### 3. Lease / reclaim — Alloy has, Dafny doesn't (carried forward)
- **Alloy evidence**: `system-models/planning_lease.als` is a self-contained model with `Alive` + `Crash` + `Reclaim` actions; this session added the heartbeat-vs-reclaim race surface (`HbSinceObs` flag, `heartbeat`/`sweepObserve` transitions, `LiveHeartbeatBlocksReclaim`, `ReclaimRequiresStableHeartbeatChain`).
- **Dafny evidence**: no `planning_lease.dfy` file exists.
- **Outcome**: **FixModel (Dafny lags)** — port `planning_lease.als` to Dafny.
- **Direction**: Create `planning_lease.dfy` paralleling `planning.dfy`'s structure. Should include both the omniscient lease (alive/crash/reclaim) AND the race-aware overlay (HbSinceObs, sweepObserve, heartbeat).
- **Effort**: Medium (~150 LOC, larger than the 04-27 estimate now that the heartbeat layer is included).

### 4. `CancelledIsRejectedTerminal` — dedicated in Alloy, implicit in Dafny (carried forward)
- **Alloy evidence**: `planning.als:421-424` is an explicit assertion `always all t: Cancelled => isRejected[t] and always isRejected[t]`.
- **Dafny evidence**: implied by combining the `Inv` clause with `TerminalAbsorbing` — semantically the same, but no dedicated lemma to find by name.
- **Outcome**: **FixModel (Dafny — presentational)** — add a thin lemma calling `InvAlwaysHolds` + `TerminalAbsorbing`.
- **Effort**: Trivial (~8 LOC).

## Aligned (no action required)

13 of 17 shared properties verify in both formalisms with equivalent semantics. The chain_predecessor migration on `main` did **not** require any model edits because both formalisms abstract storage-level race resolution into a `commitClaim | abortClaim` step split — the abstraction is broad enough to absorb mechanism changes.

## Practical reading

| Use Alloy when | Use Dafny when |
|---|---|
| You want a counterexample trace (visual, navigable) | You need confidence beyond bounded scope |
| Exploring a new layer (e.g. sticky context, lease, heartbeat race) | The property is settled and you want CI-grade coverage |
| Demonstrating a scenario to a stakeholder | Anchoring the enforcement-audit gate-claim |
| The property has temporal logic and `eventually` clauses | The property is a per-state invariant or a forall-trace property |

The two formalisms are complementary. The pattern "Alloy first, then port" is what produced this gap — sticky, lease, the heartbeat race surface, and now cycle-refusal landed in Alloy and the Dafny port hasn't caught up yet.

## Recommendations

| Action | Effort | Value |
|---|---|---|
| Add `t !in info[t].deps` requires-clause to `StepPlan` in `planning.dfy` + matching lemma | Trivial (~10 LOC) | Closes the cycle-refusal cross-formalism gap |
| Add `CancelledIsRejectedTerminal` lemma to `planning.dfy` | Trivial (~8 LOC) | Closes the presentational gap |
| Port sticky-context to `planning.dfy` | Medium (~80 LOC) | Unbounded proof for sticky safety properties |
| Port lease + heartbeat-race to a new `planning_lease.dfy` | Medium (~150 LOC) | Unbounded proof for the most operationally important safety properties |

## How to re-run

```bash
verify=/Users/a.morozov/.claude/plugins/cache/morozov-claude-plugin/formal-methods/1.3.0/skills/formal-modeling/scripts/verify.sh

# Alloy
bash $verify system-models/planning.als
bash $verify system-models/planning_lease.als
bash $verify system-models/planning_plan_race.als

# Dafny
bash $verify system-models/planning.dfy
bash $verify system-models/planning_plan_race.dfy
```

## Verdict

**Alloy and Dafny agree on the 13 shared protocol properties.** Four property families (cycle refusal, sticky context, lease/reclaim, heartbeat-vs-reclaim race) live only in Alloy — all four are bounded-scope verified there, and porting any of them to Dafny would extend confidence to unbounded trace lengths. The lease + heartbeat-race port is the highest-value missing item operationally; the cycle-refusal port is the lowest-effort.

No semantic conflict between the two formalisms exists; the gap is "Dafny has not yet caught up to four layers Alloy added", not "the models contradict".
